title: Sabulina californica
intro: Sabulina californica, also known as California sand verbena, is a small, shrubby perennial plant native to the southwestern United States. The plant is known for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to soothe digestive issues, reduce stress, and protect against cell damage. The bioactive constituents of Sabulina californica include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Sabulina californica is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to promote relaxation, improve sleep quality, and alleviate symptoms of anxiety and depression.

benefits: Sabulina californica, also known as California sand verbena, has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits. The plant is rich in antioxidants, which can help protect the body against free radicals and oxidative stress. Its extracts have been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of conditions such as arthritis. Additionally, Sabulina californica has been traditionally used to treat digestive issues, including diarrhea and nausea. More research is needed to confirm the full extent of its health benefits.
constituents: Sabulina californica contains a variety of active constituents, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenes.
